subject: Add parent for opendaylight vclients
commit_message:
Add parent for opendaylight vclients

Applications based on opendaylight need to inherit settings from the
opendaylight parent pom (odlparent).  This change adds a copy of
oparent whose parent is the odlparent pom, which will allow projects
based on opendaylight to still inherit settings from oparent.
The new odlparent-boron directory is included as module in top
level pom.xml, per review comments, to avoid release build failure.
This will have to be treated via a separate Jenkins build.

Change-Id: Icfb1f119672efc7cfd80748ee92cc331b01550a5
Issue-ID: CCSDK-43
Signed-off-by: Dan Timoney <dtimoney@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oparent-master-verify-java/34/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oparent-master-verify-java/34

Patch Set 1:

As it stands, the release-version job will fail when it sets the release version on the oparent root pom since the oparent-odlparent artifact doesn't inherit its version from the root pom.  We may need to set up a separate set of Jenkins jobs and release the oparent-odlparent artifact separately from the rest of oparent.  Any other suggestions?

If we go with the separate Jenkins jobs approach, then we should remove the inclusion of oparent-odlparent as a module in the root pom.

Patch Set 1:

I'm not sure why the release-version job would fail?  That job uses the version plugin (not release plugin) to reset the version number and executes "update-child-modules" ... so the <version> tag in child modules will also be reset.

If there's an issue I'm missing that we can work around using a separate Jenkins job, that's also fine.  I'll go with whatever approach you'd prefer. 

FYI - I actually tried just copying oparent to ccsdk/parent, but when I did that, that didn't work (it couldn't find the stylecheck pom.xml for one thing).  I was able to a local build of this change, then a local build of ccsdk/parent using this one as its parent, and then sli/core using my updated parent, and that seemed to work, but of course that's a local build.  Mileage often varies on Jenkins

Patch Set 1:

The release-version job would not update the version of oparent-odlparent, because update-child-modules only updates inherited version numbers, and oparent-odlparent doesn't inherit from the root pom.

I just verified this by running version:set against this patch locally; oparent-odlparent version is left as 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT while the other oparent artifact get there versions updated.

Patch Set 1:

The separate Jenkins jobs solution is not ideal either, since now the two sets of artifacts may go out of sync on versioning.  I can't think of a better way off the top of my head, though.

Patch Set 1:

I uploaded a change to remove the odlparent-boron module from the top pom.xml, to prevent failure of release build.  I can submit a change to ci-management to add the new Jenkins jobs for odlparent-boron if you'd like

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oparent-master-verify-java/37/ : SUCCESS

Sure, please do. Thanks.
Change has been successfully merged by Gary Wu

Patch Set 2:

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oparent-master-oparent-odlparent-release-version2-java-daily/5/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oparent-master-oparent-odlparent-release-version2-java-daily/5/ )
